framing.top_message: AI Commerce is evolving along three paths: AI reshaping how consumers buy goods, AI helping households optimize recurring expenses, and regional innovation patterns with distinctive approaches in Asia.

section_intros.retail_behavior: Retail leaders are moving from recommendation widgets to conversational buying systems. The strongest shifts occur where AI materially shortens the path from discovery to checkout.
section_intros.recurring_expense_agents_europe: European recurring-expense players are turning comparison and switching into continuous optimization journeys. The central pattern is reduced household bill leakage through guided plan and provider decisions.
section_intros.asia_innovations: Asian markets show a mobile-first, ecosystem-led model: marketplaces, super-apps, and wallets converge to support end-to-end AI-guided consumer commerce actions.

examples.what_the_agent_does: The assistant supports product search, comparison, and recommendation with price and merchant context.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Moves users from fragmented search to an integrated shortlist workflow.
examples.why_it_matters: Combines recommendation plus price/merchant signals in a payments-linked commerce ecosystem.
examples.evidence_summary: Klarna announced expanded shopping capabilities for its AI assistant powered by OpenAI.
examples.source_title: Shopping made smarter: Klarna adds more AI features to its assistant powered by OpenAI

examples.what_the_agent_does: Rufus answers product questions and helps compare alternatives using product context and customer review signals.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Reduces dependence on manual comparison tabs and long product-page reading loops.
examples.why_it_matters: Shows AI embedded into a high-traffic commerce environment with clear purchase intent.
examples.evidence_summary: Amazon describes Rufus as a generative AI shopping assistant available to help customers make informed shopping decisions.
examples.source_title: How customers are making more informed shopping decisions with Rufus

examples.what_the_agent_does: AI Mode compares products, tracks prices, and can advance to checkout with user approval.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Compresses discovery-to-purchase into one conversational loop.
examples.why_it_matters: One of the clearest mainstream examples of assisted transaction orchestration.
examples.evidence_summary: Google publicly introduced AI shopping features including agentic checkout pathways.
examples.source_title: Google Shopping launches agentic checkout and more AI shopping tools

examples.what_the_agent_does: Ask Instacart turns meal or ingredient questions into practical grocery choices and shopping actions.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Reduces meal-planning friction and accelerates conversion into basket creation.
examples.why_it_matters: Grocery is high-frequency; AI cart support has strong behavior-change potential.
examples.evidence_summary: Instacart introduced AI-powered search and conversational shopping support through Ask Instacart.
examples.source_title: Bringing Inspirational, AI-Powered Search to the Instacart app with Ask Instacart
examples.notes: If page access is restricted in some environments, use official mirrored release coverage.

examples.what_the_agent_does: Shopping Research asks clarifying questions, builds tailored product guidance, and narrows options.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Consumers delegate more of the pre-purchase research burden to AI.
examples.why_it_matters: Signals shift from search-click journeys to conversational decision orchestration.
examples.evidence_summary: OpenAI announced dedicated shopping research flows in ChatGPT for product decisions.
examples.source_title: Introducing shopping research in ChatGPT

examples.what_the_agent_does: Perplexity supports product discovery and checkout progression for eligible users and merchants.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Reduces channel switching between search, comparison, and final purchase.
examples.why_it_matters: Early example of AI answer engines integrating commerce transaction flows.
examples.evidence_summary: Reuters reported Perplexity adding shopping features including Buy with Pro.
examples.source_title: AI startup Perplexity adds shopping features as search competition tightens

examples.what_the_agent_does: Visa provides standards for trusted AI agent transactions with explicit user consent and payment controls.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Enables consumers to delegate transaction steps while retaining guardrails.
examples.why_it_matters: Payment-layer trust and authorization are critical for scaled agentic commerce.
examples.evidence_summary: Visa publicly unveiled protocol-level infrastructure for AI commerce and trusted agent interactions.
examples.source_title: Visa Unveils Trusted Agent Protocol for AI Commerce

examples.what_the_agent_does: Sparky helps users ask shopping questions, narrow options, and move from broad intent to a practical shortlist.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Shifts behavior from category browsing to conversational selection with reduced search effort.
examples.why_it_matters: Large-scale proof that a mass retailer can embed AI guidance directly into shopping intent flows.
examples.evidence_summary: Walmart positions Sparky as an agentic shopping assistant focused on customer product discovery and decision support.
examples.source_title: Walmart: The Future of Shopping Is Agentic. Meet Sparky.

examples.what_the_agent_does: Muse converts style intent and room context into visualized, shoppable product suggestions.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Turns inspiration browsing into guided selection with immediate commerce paths.
examples.why_it_matters: Home retail is high-consideration; AI helps move shoppers from ideation to transaction faster.
examples.evidence_summary: Wayfair has publicly described AI-driven inspiration and shopping workflows through Muse and related tools.
examples.source_title: Wayfair Introduces New AI-Powered Tool 'Muse'

examples.what_the_agent_does: Papernest helps households compare, move, and manage recurring contracts such as energy, internet, and insurance.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Shifts recurring expense management from fragmented provider journeys to a centralized optimization flow.
examples.why_it_matters: Demonstrates consumer demand for delegated contract administration in Europe.
examples.evidence_summary: Papernest publicly positions itself as a one-stop service for switching and managing household contracts.
examples.source_title: papernest, LA solution pour gérer tous vos contrats

examples.what_the_agent_does: Selectra compares and facilitates switching across energy, telecom, and related recurring services in multiple European markets.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Makes recurring spend optimization a routine behavior through guided switching paths.
examples.why_it_matters: Illustrates Europe-wide consumer appetite for bill and tariff optimization intermediaries.
examples.evidence_summary: Selectra publicly documents large-scale multi-country comparison and contract-signing activity.
examples.source_title: Selectra group | Corporate website page

examples.what_the_agent_does: Magenta AI sits in customer app/device journeys and supports discovery of telecom-linked offers and bundles.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Transitions plan and service exploration toward AI-assisted telecom ecosystems.
examples.why_it_matters: Important telco example of AI integrated into recurring service relationships.
examples.evidence_summary: Deutsche Telekom publicly describes Magenta AI and AI-phone experiences connected to consumer offerings.
examples.source_title: “Our” AI-phone brings „AI for everyone“ | Deutsche Telekom

examples.what_the_agent_does: ChatBarry asks usage questions and recommends better-fit mobile plans.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Encourages consumers to reassess and optimize recurring telco plans more frequently.
examples.why_it_matters: Directly targets persistent bill leakage in mobile subscriptions.
examples.evidence_summary: Goji presents ChatBarry as an AI matchmaker for phone-plan comparison and selection.
examples.source_title: Meet ChatBarry: The AI Matchmaker for Phone Plans

examples.what_the_agent_does: The app brings policy and deal comparison into conversational prompts, reducing manual comparison effort.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Household bill optimization starts in AI chat rather than in long form journeys.
examples.why_it_matters: Represents AI-enabled migration of classic comparison behavior into conversational interfaces.
examples.evidence_summary: Industry coverage describes MoneySuperMarket's AI-powered comparison integration in ChatGPT.
examples.source_title: MoneySuperMarket brings AI-powered comparison to ChatGPT

examples.what_the_agent_does: Switcha positions AI-driven price comparison to identify better-value recurring household offers.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Promotes continuous optimization rather than annual manual switching behavior.
examples.why_it_matters: Signals a newer wave of AI-native bill-comparison experiences in Europe.
examples.evidence_summary: Switcha publicly markets itself as an AI-powered price-comparison savings platform.
examples.source_title: Switcha | AI-Powered Price Comparison for Smarter Savings
examples.notes: Treat as emerging player; external validation depth is lower than large incumbents.

examples.what_the_agent_does: TOBi handles customer support and account questions relevant to recurring mobile and broadband usage.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Keeps recurring plan-management interactions in always-on digital channels.
examples.why_it_matters: Shows telecom customer-management assistants evolving toward recurring-expense journey support.
examples.evidence_summary: Vodafone presents TOBi as a 24/7 digital assistant for account and service interactions.
examples.source_title: Just Ask Once | Contact us | Vodafone UK
examples.notes: Primarily support-led; included for recurring service journey relevance.

examples.what_the_agent_does: Taobao Wenwen supports conversational product recommendations, deal analysis, and product pros/cons summaries.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Shortens decision loops in China's high-frequency digital retail ecosystem.
examples.why_it_matters: A large-scale benchmark for AI-mediated consumer buying behavior in Asia.
examples.evidence_summary: Alibaba describes Wenwen as integrated consumer AI for recommendation and shopping decisions.
examples.source_title: Taobao and Tmall Upgrades Consumer Shopping Experience and Merchant Support Through AI

examples.what_the_agent_does: Qwen App coordinates shopping, food orders, travel booking, and payments through one conversational layer.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Moves consumers from app-switching behavior toward unified intent-to-action execution.
examples.why_it_matters: Represents one of the strongest super-app agentic commerce patterns globally.
examples.evidence_summary: Alibaba Cloud and Reuters coverage describe Qwen's integrated execution across Taobao, Alipay, and Fliggy.
examples.source_title: Alibaba’s Qwen App Advances Agentic AI Strategy

examples.what_the_agent_does: AskMe converts travel intent into itinerary options and booking steps tied to live travel inventory.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Reduces friction between discovery and booking for multi-step travel purchases.
examples.why_it_matters: Travel is a high-complexity category where agentic assistance has outsized conversion impact.
examples.evidence_summary: Fliggy-related public releases describe AI-led itinerary generation and booking enablement.
examples.source_title: Fliggy Launches AI Travel Assistant 'AskMe'

examples.what_the_agent_does: JD's AI capabilities include shopper support, recommendation, and AI-led merchant-consumer interaction improvements.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Supports faster product matching and less manual browsing in large-catalog commerce.
examples.why_it_matters: Shows intensifying AI-commerce competition among major Chinese marketplaces.
examples.evidence_summary: JD communications and sector coverage highlight AI shopping and support capabilities for e-commerce use cases.
examples.source_title: JD.com Announces Enhanced Spring Dawn Initiative with New AI Service Package
examples.notes: Some capabilities are merchant-facing but have direct consumer journey impact.

examples.what_the_agent_does: Paytm's AI features include spend categorization and payment-assistant capabilities that help users monitor recurring outflows.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Moves users toward proactive expense management within everyday payment journeys.
examples.why_it_matters: India-scale wallet ecosystems can normalize AI-guided recurring spend behaviors quickly.
examples.evidence_summary: Paytm publicly described AI-first app features for payment intelligence and spend organization.
examples.source_title: Paytm launches all-new app with AI-first features

examples.what_the_agent_does: Rakuten AI supports natural-language shopping intent and personalized product recommendation on Rakuten Ichiba.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Encourages consumers to use AI concierge style discovery over manual category navigation.
examples.why_it_matters: Important Japan market signal for agentic marketplaces.
examples.evidence_summary: Rakuten announced roll-out of Rakuten AI shopping capabilities in Ichiba app experiences.
examples.source_title: Rakuten launches agentic AI tools in Rakuten Ichiba app

examples.what_the_agent_does: Sea and Google are exploring agentic shopping prototypes for Shopee to streamline search-to-purchase tasks.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Indicates move toward delegated multi-step purchase support in SEA mobile commerce.
examples.why_it_matters: Demonstrates that agentic commerce patterns are extending to Southeast Asia's largest marketplaces.
examples.evidence_summary: Reuters reported collaboration between Google and Sea on AI agentic shopping tools.
examples.source_title: Google, Shopee-owner Sea to develop AI tools for e-commerce, gaming

examples.what_the_agent_does: TripGenie provides conversational itinerary support and connects options to bookable travel inventory.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Increases consumer willingness to plan and book travel through AI-first flows.
examples.why_it_matters: One of the clearer Asia-headquartered travel examples at global scale.
examples.evidence_summary: Trip.com describes TripGenie as an AI assistant for planning and booking journeys.
examples.source_title: Introducing TripGenie: AI travel assistant by Trip.com

examples.what_the_agent_does: Kakao combines shopping discovery signals and AI assistant patterns inside core messaging and commerce surfaces.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Blends social context and shopping recommendation, reducing decision friction.
examples.why_it_matters: Shows how Asian chat ecosystems integrate commerce recommendation natively.
examples.evidence_summary: Kakao service and market coverage highlight AI shopping-agent ambitions in Kakao ecosystem.
examples.source_title: Start shopping smart with Shopping How | Kakao

examples.what_the_agent_does: Naver's shopping agent uses conversational queries and preference signals to recommend suitable products.
examples.buying_behavior_impact: Shifts shopping behavior from keyword search to iterative AI-guided exploration.
examples.why_it_matters: South Korea's commerce platforms are rapidly integrating specialized shopping LLMs.
examples.evidence_summary: Public Korea coverage describes Naver's beta shopping AI agent and category expansion plans.
examples.source_title: Naver launches shopping AI agent beta
